const AllVariants = () => <Link
    href="/variants"
    size="large"
    weight="bold"
    color="success"
    onContainer="secondary"
    wrap="wrap"
    underline="always"
    truncate={false}
    target="_blank"
    rel="noopener noreferrer">All Variants Link</Link>;

    return (
        <Accordion.Root>
            <Accordion.Item value="accordion-test-0">

border.description:

defaultValue.description: The items whose contents are expanded on initial render. Use when you do not need to control accordion state.

value.description: The controlled value of the items whose contents are expanded.

padding.description:
Controls padding around the header. `'none'` for no padding, `'small'`, `'medium'`, or `'large'` for increasing spacing.
@default 'large'

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when the expanded items change.

emphasis.description:
Controls visual weight. Use `'strong'` for high-priority information, `'weak'` for subtle or secondary content.
@default 'strong'

variant.description:
Conveys the tone. `'default'` for neutral, `'positive'` for success, `'highlight'` for informational, `'warning'` for caution, `'negative'` for critical.
@default 'default'

size.description:
Controls the badge size. Use `'small'` when space is limited or the badge plays a secondary role.
@default 'default'

disabled.description:
Prevents interaction and dims the badge visually.
@default false

onClick.description: Click handler. When provided, the badge renders as a button element.

onDismiss.description: Dismiss handler. When provided, a dismiss button is shown, useful for filters or removable selections.

status.description: Displays a status dot to reflect live states or activity. Accepts a preset (`'positive'`, `'warning'`, etc.) or a custom color value.

title.description: Tooltip text shown on hover. Also used as the accessible name when no `aria-label` is provided.

children.description: The badge content — typically short text, a number, or a label.

type.description:
The HTML button type. Use `'submit'` for form submission, `'reset'` to clear form fields, `'button'` for general actions.
@default "button"

title.description: Tooltip text shown on hover. Also used as the accessible name when no `aria-label` is provided.

variant.description:
Conveys the tone. `'default'` for neutral actions, `'positive'` for confirmations like "Approve", `'negative'` for cautionary actions like "Cancel", `'danger'` for irreversible actions like "Delete", `'loud'` for high-visibility actions — use sparingly.
@default "default"

emphasis.description:
Controls visual weight. `'strong'` for primary actions, `'default'` for common actions, `'weak'` for secondary or less prominent options.
@default "strong"

size.description:
Controls the button size. `'small'` for tight spaces or lower priority, `'medium'` for most layouts, `'large'` for prominent actions.
@default "medium"

rounding.description:
Controls border radius. `'medium'` for standard buttons, `'full'` for pill-shaped buttons.
@default "medium"

disabled.description:
Prevents interaction and dims the button visually.
@default false

aspect.description:
Controls the aspect ratio. `'default'` for standard buttons with label, `'square'` for icon-only buttons.
@default "default"

hugWidth.description:
When `true`, the button sizes to fit its content. When `false`, the button expands to fill its container width. Use full-width sparingly — it works well on mobile or at the bottom of forms.
@default true

children.description: The button content — text, icons, or a combination of both.

onPress.description: Callback fired when the button is pressed.

form.description: Associates the button with a `<form>` by its `id`. Use when the button sits outside the form element in the DOM.

defaultValue.description:
The initial checked state for uncontrolled usage. Pass `'indeterminate'` for a mixed state (e.g., when some but not all child items are selected).
@default false

value.description:
The controlled checked state. Use together with `onChange` for controlled usage. Pass `'indeterminate'` for a mixed state.
@default false

size.description:
Controls the checkbox size. `'default'` fits most layouts, `'large'` for touch-friendly environments or increased visual weight.
@default "default"

emphasis.description:
Controls visual weight. `'default'` is subtle for standard forms, `'weak'` for reduced visual weight.
@default "default"

disabled.description:
Prevents interaction and dims the checkbox visually.
@default false

required.description:
Marks the checkbox as required for form submission. The indicator should appear next to the label.
@default false

readOnly.description:
Makes the checkbox read-only. The checkbox is visible and focusable but its value cannot be changed by user interaction.
@default false

status.description:
Visual status of the checkbox. Use `'error'` to indicate a validation failure.
@default "default"

onChange.description: Callback fired when the checked state changes.

onBlur.description: Callback fired when the checkbox loses focus.

onFocus.description: Callback fired when the checkbox gains focus.

variant.description:
Controls the line style. `'solid'` for a continuous line, `'dashed'` for a dotted line, `'noline'` for spacing-only separation.
@default "solid"

padding.description:
Controls the spacing around the divider. `'none'` for tight layouts, `'medium'` for standard separation, `'large'` for prominent breaks.
@default "medium"

color.description:
Controls the line color intensity. `'weak'` for subtle separation, `'default'` for standard, `'strong'` for prominent borders.
@default "default"

direction.description:
Controls the orientation. `'horizontal'` for full-width breaks, `'vertical'` for side-by-side content separation.
@default "horizontal"

as.description:
The HTML element to render. Use `'li'` when the divider sits inside a list.
@default "div"

decorative.description:
When `true`, the divider is purely visual — it carries no semantic meaning and is hidden from the accessibility tree.
@default false

shadow.description:
Controls the drop shadow. `'none'` for flat layouts, `'medium'` for standard elevation, `'large'` for prominent overlays.
@default "medium"

rounded.description:
Controls the border radius. `'none'` for sharp edges, `'medium'` for standard, `'large'` for more rounded corners.
@default "medium"

side.description:
The preferred side the flyout opens on. Falls back to the opposite side if there isn't enough viewport space.
@default "bottom"

align.description:
Controls the horizontal alignment of the flyout relative to the trigger.
@default "start"

padding.description:
Controls the inner padding of the flyout content.
@default "compact"

width.description:
Sets a fixed width for the flyout. Accepts any CSS length value.
@default "fit-content"

triggerOffset.description:
Controls the spacing between the flyout and its trigger.
@default 'compact'

maxWidth.description:
The maximum width of the flyout. Accepts any CSS length value.
@default "360px"

viewportCollisionPadding.description:
Controls the minimum distance between the flyout and the viewport edge.
@default 'compact'

onOpenAutoFocus.description: Callback fired when the flyout auto-focuses on open. Call `event.preventDefault()` to take manual control of focus.

onEscapeKeyDown.description: Callback fired when the Escape key is pressed while the flyout is open.

variant.description:
Conveys the tone. `'default'` for neutral messages, `'highlight'` for noteworthy information, `'positive'` for success, `'warning'` for caution, `'danger'` for critical errors — use sparingly.
@default 'default'

emphasis.description:
Controls visual weight. `'default'` for standard messages, `'strong'` for critical or high-priority information, `'weak'` for subtle, non-urgent messages.
@default 'default'

size.description:
Controls the notice size. `'medium'` for standard use, `'large'` for more prominent messages.
@default 'medium'

icon.description: Leading icon element to enhance recognizability and convey the notice's function at a glance.

action.description: Action element (typically a Button). Use when there's a clear, lightweight action related to the message content.

onDismiss.description: Callback when the dismiss button is clicked. When provided, a close icon is displayed allowing users to remove the message.

aria-label.description: Custom accessible label for the dismiss button. Overrides the default "Dismiss" text.

children.description: The message content — text, links, or formatted content describing the notice.

    Button,

layout.description:
Controls how column widths are calculated. `'auto'` sizes columns to fit their content, `'fixed'` distributes width evenly for predictable layouts.
@default 'auto'

fontSize.description:
Controls the text size of table content. `'small'` for dense data tables, `'medium'` for standard readability.
@default 'medium'

gutter.description:
Additional spacing between table cells (both horizontal and vertical)

This value is added to the default cell spacing. For example, with `gutter="48px"`,
cells will have 48px of additional space between them in both directions
(24px added to each side).

Accepts any CSS length value with units (e.g., '16px', '1rem', '48px')

**Important:** Always include a unit, even for zero (use '0px', not '0')
@default '0px'

sticky.description:
Whether header should stick to the top when scrolling
@deprecated Use `Table.Header sticky` prop instead. For sticky columns, use `Table.Body firstColumnSticky` or `lastColumnSticky` props

noBorder.description:
Removes the top and bottom borders. Useful when the table sits inside a container that already provides visual separation.
@default false

@default "medium"

Callback fired when the active tab changes.
@param value - The value of the newly active tab

variant.description:
Controls the visual style. `'default'` for underline-style tabs, `'pill'` for filled pill-shaped tabs.
@default "default"

withDivider.description:
Add a divider line below the tabs
Only available when variant is 'pill'

components-text-input.import: import { Button, Flex, IconIcon, IconPen, Label, TextFieldRoot, TextInput } from "@frontify/fondue-components";

children.description: Slot content placed alongside the input. Use `TextInput.Slot` for left/right decorators or actions.

defaultValue.description: The initial value for uncontrolled usage. Accepts a string or number.

value.description: The controlled value. Use together with `onChange` for controlled usage.

type.description:
The HTML input type. Use `'password'` for secrets, `'email'` for email addresses, `'number'` for numeric input, `'search'` for search fields.
@default "text"

placeholder.description: Text shown when the input is empty. Use it to hint at the expected input.

disabled.description:
Prevents interaction and dims the input visually.
@default false

required.description:
Marks the input as required for form validation.
@default false

spellCheck.description:
Enables browser spell-checking for the input content.
@default true

readOnly.description:
Makes the input read-only — the value is visible but cannot be edited.
@default false

autoComplete.description:
Controls the browser's autocomplete behavior. Pass a specific token like `'email'`, `'username'`, or `'off'` to disable.
@default "on"

maxLength.description: The maximum number of characters the user can enter.

status.description:
Conveys validation state visually. `'success'` shows a checkmark, `'error'` shows a warning icon, `'loading'` shows a spinner.
@default "neutral"

onChange.description: Callback fired when the input value changes.

onBlur.description: Callback fired when the input loses focus.

onFocus.description: Callback fired when the input gains focus.

onKeyDown.description: Callback fired when a key is pressed inside the input.

onKeyUp.description: Callback fired when a key is released inside the input.

onSelect.description: Callback fired when the user selects text inside the input.

children.description: Slot content placed alongside the textarea. Use `Textarea.Slot` for left/right decorators or actions.

autocomplete.description: Enables browser autocomplete suggestions for the textarea.

autosize.description: When `true`, the textarea grows automatically as the user types, eliminating the need for manual resizing.

clearable.description: Shows a clear button that empties the textarea on click.

decorator.description: A React element rendered at the start of the textarea — typically an icon or label prefix.

defaultValue.description: The initial value for uncontrolled usage.

disabled.description:
Prevents interaction and dims the textarea visually.
@default false

elements.name: signature

extraActions.description:
Collection of extra actions the input can preform
@deprecated Use Textarea.Slot instead for custom actions

focusOnMount.description: When `true`, the textarea receives focus automatically when the component mounts.

minRows.description:
The minimum number of visible text rows. Only applies when `autosize` is `false`.
@default 1

maxRows.description: The maximum number of visible text rows before scrolling activates. Only applies when `autosize` is `true`.

onChange.description: Callback fired when the textarea value changes.

onBlur.description: Callback fired when the textarea loses focus.

onFocus.description: Callback fired when the textarea gains focus.

onKeyDown.description: Callback fired when a key is pressed inside the textarea.

onEnterPressed.description: Callback fired specifically when the Enter key is pressed. Useful for submit-on-enter behavior.

onKeyUp.description: Callback fired when a key is released inside the textarea.

onSelect.description: Callback fired when the user selects text inside the textarea.

required.description: Marks the textarea as required for form validation.

placeholder.description: Text shown when the textarea is empty. Use it to hint at the expected input.

readOnly.description: Makes the textarea read-only — the value is visible but cannot be edited.

resizable.description: When `true`, shows a drag handle for manual resizing.

selectable.description:
When `false`, prevents the user from selecting text inside the textarea.
@default true

status.description:
Conveys the validation state visually. `'success'` shows a checkmark, `'error'` shows a warning icon, `'loading'` shows a progress indicator.
@default 'default'

value.description: The controlled value. Use together with `onChange` for controlled usage.
